summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates SSH keys and host data to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes attacker-controlled reconnaissance and credential theft against the installer. The script shells out to enumerate /etc/hosts, /etc/resolv.conf, network interfaces, and `whoami`/`hostname` output; recursively reads every file under /root/.ssh and cats the contents; greps the filesystem for `HTB{...}` flag strings; and uses a hardcoded bearer token (`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`) to query an internal Verdaccio at registry.spectral-corsair.htb:1337 (whoami, /-/all, /-/v1/search, package dumps). The collected output is base64-encoded and PUT as JSON to http://154.57.164.79:30749/api/modules/ECT-* (with a localhost fallback at 127.0.0.1:32271). The payload is gated to fire only in a targeted environment: it skips execution under CI, GitHub Actions, and scanner-mirror npm registries (npmmirror, tencent, taobao, hscan, ustc, huawei, cnpm), and requires both a 12-hex Docker-style hostname and a cwd under /app — explicit sandbox-evasion shape consistent with purpose-built malware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
